steps.title: Let's set up a Q&A slide!
steps.description: A Q&A slide turns any presentation into a two-way conversation — your audience sends questions from their phones and you answer them live. We'll build one and tour all the key settings together. Click Next to begin.

steps.title: Create a new presentation
steps.description: Click 'New Presentation' to open a fresh deck.

steps.title: Pick the Q&A slide type
steps.description: Select 'Q&A' from the slide type picker — it gathers audience questions in real time.

steps.title: Write your Q&A heading
steps.description: Type a heading that invites your audience to ask — something clear and welcoming, so participants know exactly what this slide is for.

action.hint: Type your Q&A heading, then click away

steps.title: When can the audience ask?
steps.description: Choose whether questions arrive only on this dedicated Q&A slide, or on every slide throughout your presentation. 'Only on Q&A slides' gives you tighter control of the timing.

steps.title: Filter profanity
steps.description: Tick 'Filter profanity' to automatically block swear words in submitted questions — ideal for school events or public audiences.

steps.title: Moderate questions before they appear
steps.description: Enable 'Moderate audience's questions' to approve each question privately before it shows on screen. A dedicated moderation link lets you or a co-host filter questions in a separate tab.

steps.title: Let the audience see each other's questions
steps.description: When on, each audience member can see all submitted questions on their own device — great for transparency and encouraging upvotes.

steps.title: Allow anonymous questions
steps.description: Turn this on to let participants skip entering their name. It encourages more candid questions in sensitive topics or team retrospectives.

steps.title: Ready to go live!
steps.description: That's your Q&A slide set up with all the right settings. Hit 'Present' and your audience starts sending questions on their phones — you organise and answer them all from one screen.
